query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
(360011, 'What does the term "prolegomena" mean in theological studies?', 'multiple_choice', '["Introduction to church history","First things or preliminary matters","Study of prophecy","Analysis of biblical languages"]', 'First things or preliminary matters', 1),
(360011, 'According to the lesson, what is the primary source of theological knowledge?', 'multiple_choice', '["Human reason alone","Church tradition exclusively","Divine revelation in Scripture","Philosophical speculation"]', 'Divine revelation in Scripture', 2),
(360011, 'What Hebrew term conveys relational and experiential knowledge of God?', 'multiple_choice', '["Logos","Daat (Knowledge)","Theos","Kyrios"]', 'Daat (Knowledge)', 3),
(360011, 'In John 1:1-14, the Greek term logos is identified with:', 'multiple_choice', '["The Holy Spirit","The pre-existent Christ","The written Scripture","The prophets"]', 'The pre-existent Christ', 4),
(360011, 'What is the significance of Genesis 1:1-3 for theological method?', 'multiple_choice', '["It establishes the primacy of divine speech in revelation","It proves the age of the earth","It describes the fall of humanity","It introduces the law of Moses"]', 'It establishes the primacy of divine speech in revelation', 5),
(360011, 'Which passage serves as a doxological reflection on God''s inscrutable wisdom?', 'multiple_choice', '["Genesis 1:1-3","John 1:1-14","Romans 11:33-36","Exodus 3:14"]', 'Romans 11:33-36', 6),
(360011, 'The ancient Near Eastern context helps us understand:', 'multiple_choice', '["The uniqueness of Israel''s monotheistic faith","Greek philosophy exclusively","Medieval church practices","Modern scientific theories"]', 'The uniqueness of Israel''s monotheistic faith', 7),
(360011, 'What approach does the lesson recommend for theological inquiry?', 'multiple_choice', '["Purely rationalistic speculation","Reverent engagement with divine self-disclosure","Rejection of historical context","Reliance on personal experience alone"]', 'Reverent engagement with divine self-disclosure', 8),
(360011, 'The Nicene Creed and Chalcedonian Definition are examples of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ancient Near Eastern mythology","Historical creedal formulations","Modern theological innovations","Gnostic teachings"]', 'Historical creedal formulations', 9),
(360011, 'Why is prolegomena essential for pastoral ministry?', 'multiple_choice', '["It provides entertainment for congregations","It establishes methodological principles for faithful teaching","It replaces the need for Scripture study","It focuses only on academic debates"]', 'It establishes methodological principles for faithful teaching', 10),
(360011, 'Explain how the study of prolegomena serves as a foundation for systematic theology and why it matters for pastoral ministry.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The study of prolegomena establishes the methodological principles necessary for systematic theology', 11);

(360011, 'The ancient Near Eastern context helps us understand:', 'multiple_choice', '["The uniqueness of Israel''s monotheistic faith","Greek philosophy exclusively","Medieval church practices","Modern scientific theories"]', 'The uniqueness of Israel''s monotheistic faith', 7),

(360011, 'Explain how the study of prolegomena serves as a foundation for systematic theology and why it matters for pastoral ministry.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The study of prolegomena establishes the methodological principles necessary for systematic theology', 11);
